description: Add null guards for GraphQL response fields across all Linear tools and fix Retry-After header parsing.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/linear && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add null guards for GraphQL query response fields in Linear tools
userStories.description: As a user, I want Linear tools to return helpful ToolError.notFound errors instead of crashing with TypeError when a queried resource doesn't exist.
acceptanceCriteria: list-comments.ts guards data.issue before accessing .comments, throwing ToolError.notFound if null
list-cycles.ts guards data.team before accessing .cycles, throwing ToolError.notFound if null
list-workflow-states.ts guards data.team before accessing .states, throwing ToolError.notFound if null
search-issues.ts guards data.searchIssues (line 71) and data.issues (line 98) before accessing .nodes
list-projects.ts guards data.projects before accessing .nodes
list-users.ts guards data.users before accessing .nodes
ToolError is imported from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk in all affected files
cd plugins/linear && npm run build passes
cd plugins/linear && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/linear && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/linear && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
The Linear plugin uses a graphql() function that returns `result.data` as the typed T. However, GraphQL can return null for top-level fields when a resource doesn't exist (e.g., `{ data: { issue: null } }` when querying a deleted issue). The graphql() function only checks `if (!result.data)` but not inner fields.

All query tools access nested fields without null checks, which crashes with TypeError. Fix each by adding a guard after the graphql() call:

1. plugins/linear/src/tools/list-comments.ts line 46:
   `const result = data.issue.comments;` → add `if (!data.issue) throw ToolError.notFound('Issue not found');` before this line

2. plugins/linear/src/tools/list-cycles.ts line 45:
   `const result = data.team.cycles;` → add `if (!data.team) throw ToolError.notFound('Team not found');` before

3. plugins/linear/src/tools/list-workflow-states.ts line 37:
   `const nodes = data.team.states.nodes` → add `if (!data.team) throw ToolError.notFound('Team not found');` before

4. plugins/linear/src/tools/search-issues.ts:
   - Line 71: `const result = data.searchIssues;` → add `if (!data.searchIssues) throw ToolError.internal('Search returned no results object');`
   - Line 98: `const result = data.issues;` → add `if (!data.issues) throw ToolError.internal('Issues query returned no results object');`

5. plugins/linear/src/tools/list-projects.ts line 43:
   `const result = data.projects;` → add `if (!data.projects) throw ToolError.internal('Projects query returned no results object');`

6. plugins/linear/src/tools/list-users.ts line 40:
   `const result = data.users;` → add `if (!data.users) throw ToolError.internal('Users query returned no results object');`

For entity-specific queries (issue, team), use ToolError.notFound. For collection queries (searchIssues, issues, projects, users), use ToolError.internal since these should always return a connection object.

userStories.title: Add null guards for GraphQL mutation response fields in Linear tools
userStories.description: As a user, I want Linear mutation tools to return helpful errors instead of crashing with TypeError when a mutation fails silently.
acceptanceCriteria: create-issue.ts guards data.issueCreate before accessing .issue, throwing ToolError.internal if null
create-comment.ts guards data.commentCreate before accessing .comment, throwing ToolError.internal if null
update-issue.ts guards data.issueUpdate before accessing .issue, throwing ToolError.internal if null
delete-issue.ts guards data.issueDelete before accessing .success, throwing ToolError.internal if null
ToolError is imported from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk in all affected files
cd plugins/linear && npm run build passes
cd plugins/linear && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/linear && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/linear && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Same root cause as US-001 but for mutation tools. GraphQL mutations can return null for the mutation field if permissions fail silently or the operation is rejected without raising a GraphQL error.

1. plugins/linear/src/tools/create-issue.ts line 72:
   `return { issue: mapIssue(data.issueCreate.issue ...) };` → add `if (!data.issueCreate?.issue) throw ToolError.internal('Issue creation failed — no issue returned');` before

2. plugins/linear/src/tools/create-comment.ts line 38:
   `return { comment: mapComment(data.commentCreate.comment ...) };` → add `if (!data.commentCreate?.comment) throw ToolError.internal('Comment creation failed — no comment returned');` before

3. plugins/linear/src/tools/update-issue.ts line 69:
   `return { issue: mapIssue(data.issueUpdate.issue ...) };` → add `if (!data.issueUpdate?.issue) throw ToolError.internal('Issue update failed — no issue returned');` before

4. plugins/linear/src/tools/delete-issue.ts line 29:
   `return { success: data.issueDelete.success };` → add `if (!data.issueDelete) throw ToolError.internal('Issue deletion failed — no response');` before

userStories.title: Use SDK parseRetryAfterMs in Linear API caller
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the Linear plugin to correctly parse Retry-After headers in both seconds and HTTP-date formats so that AI agents receive valid retry timing metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Line ~192 in linear-api.ts uses parseRetryAfterMs from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk instead of `Number(retryAfter) * 1000`
The import for parseRetryAfterMs is added to the existing @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk import line
HTTP-date format Retry-After values are correctly parsed instead of producing NaN
cd plugins/linear && npm run build passes
cd plugins/linear && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/linear && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/linear && npm run format:check passes
